title: Task Scheduler
problem_statement: Schedule tasks so the same type is separated by at least n intervals, minimizing total intervals including idle time.

examples.explanation: One optimal schedule is A B idle A B idle A B.

intuition: Always execute the most frequent available task when possible, and insert idle intervals only when forced by the cooldown.

approaches.name: Max-heap with cooldown
approaches.description: Use a max-heap of task counts and temporarily hold tasks in a cooldown queue for n intervals before reusing them.
approaches.why_not: Simple greedy without cooldown tracking may violate the required gap between same tasks.

interview_qa.a: The maximum count of a task and the required cooldown intervals.

interview_qa.q: Why may idle time be needed?
interview_qa.a: Because the same task cannot execute again until the cooldown has passed.
